title: CCPA/CPRA — Minor Consent — Marketing Cookie Fires Before Parental Consent Timestamp

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → '§1798.120'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=web sourcetype="consent:web" earliest=-30d
| eval pc=strptime(parental_consent_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=web sourcetype="tag:fire" marketing_flag=true earliest=-30d
    | fields session_id, tag_vendor, _time
  ]
| where _time < pc OR isnull(pc)
| stats count by tag_vendor, session_id
| sort - count
value: Tracking technologies on minor-directed experiences require heightened controls. Tag-manager events that load ad or analytics pixels prior to recorded parental consent timestamps evidence misconfiguration of consent mode integrations.
implementation: (1) Scope to minor-designated site IDs via lookup `minor_site_ids.csv`; (2) partner with marketing ops on tag inventory; (3) GPC and CMP linkage testing in pre-prod; (4) document vendor contracts; (5) evidence for AG inquiries on dark pattern adjacent issues.
visualization: Table (sessions), Bar by vendor, Single value (violations).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
